phases.label: Phase 1 — AI Essentials
phases.nextStepDescription: Recommend one Phase 2 module that best fits the learner's current gaps.

modules.title: How AI Actually Works
modules.description: Token generation, training vs. inference, parameters and scale, and what the app around the model actually does.

questions.question: Walk me through what happens when you type a message and an AI responds — from your input to the output, token by token.
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how tokenization, next-token probabilities, and sampling work together during generation.

questions.question: What's the difference between training and inference? Why does it matter for how you use AI tools day-to-day?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review why deployed models use frozen weights and what that means for knowledge cutoffs and daily tool use.

questions.question: What is a parameter (weight) in an LLM? What does 'a 70B model' mean, and why does parameter count matter?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review what parameters encode and why scale affects both capability and compute requirements.

questions.shortTitle: The AI harness and agentic loop
questions.question: Describe what an AI harness does. When you type a message into an app like Claude, what happens before and after the language model itself is called?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how the app assembles context from memory, instructions, and skills before calling the model, and how tool calls and the observe loop work during multi-step tasks.

modules.title: Using AI Effectively
modules.description: Context windows, hallucination and grounding, and the prompting techniques that get better answers.

questions.question: What is a context window, and what are two practical ways it affects how you use AI tools?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review the context window limit, the lost-in-the-middle problem, and how to manage long conversations or large documents.

questions.question: What is hallucination, and what's the most reliable technique for reducing it when you need factually accurate output?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review why source-grounded generation reduces hallucination more reliably than relying on model recall alone.

questions.question: Explain the difference between zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting. When would you use each?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review when to use zero-shot, few-shot, and step-by-step reasoning patterns for different task types.

modules.title: AI in Your Workday
modules.description: Agents, prompt injection, safety, and knowing when a human needs to stay in the loop.

questions.question: What is an agent, and what makes it different from a single prompt-response interaction?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how agents combine model reasoning, tool calls, observations, and iteration to complete tasks.

questions.question: What is prompt injection, and can you give an example of an indirect injection attack in a real work context?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how indirect prompt injection can arrive through documents, emails, web pages, or other untrusted content the AI reads.

questions.question: When should an AI agent always require human approval before taking an action? Use the 'blast radius' framing.
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how blast radius and reversibility determine where agents need approval checkpoints.

phases.label: Phase 2 — Claude in Practice
phases.nextStepDescription: Recommend one Phase 3 module that best fits the learner's current gaps or interests.

modules.title: Getting Started with Claude
modules.description: The Claude app, writing a good first message, attaching files, choosing a model, and chat hygiene.

questions.question: You need Claude's help with a real task — say, preparing a summary of a long report for your team. What would you put in your first message, and why does each part matter?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review the goal–context–format pattern for first messages and why attaching the real document beats describing it.

questions.question: When should you start a new chat instead of continuing an existing one? What tends to go wrong in very long conversations — and what does the app quietly do when a chat outgrows the context window?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review why long mixed-topic chats degrade answer quality and when a fresh chat with a clean summary works better.

questions.question: What kinds of things can you attach to a Claude chat, and why is attaching the actual file usually better than describing its contents? When might you switch which model you're using?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review which file types Claude can read, why real attachments ground its answers, and when to pick a faster vs. more capable model.

modules.title: Projects, Memory & Custom Instructions
modules.description: Projects, project knowledge, custom instructions, personal preferences, and styles.

questions.question: What is a Project in Claude, and when would you create one instead of just starting a regular chat?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review what a Project gives you over a plain chat and which kinds of recurring work justify creating one.

questions.question: What belongs in a Project's knowledge and instructions, and how does it change the answers you get in every chat inside that Project?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review what reference material and instructions to load into a Project so every chat starts already informed.

questions.question: What's the difference between your personal preferences (which apply everywhere) and a Project's instructions? Give an example of something that belongs in each.
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review the difference between account-wide preferences, per-Project instructions, and per-chat requests — and what belongs at each layer.

modules.title: Creating Documents & Artifacts
modules.description: Artifacts — drafting, iterating on, and exporting real documents, slides, and more.

questions.question: What is an artifact in Claude, and why is it more useful than getting the same content inside a chat reply?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review what artifacts are, when Claude creates one, and why a separate working document beats content buried in chat replies.

questions.question: You asked Claude for a first draft and it's about 70% right. What does a strong revision workflow look like from there?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how to ask for targeted changes to specific sections rather than regenerating whole drafts, and how to steer tone and structure.

questions.question: How do you get work out of Claude and into the format your team actually needs — a document, spreadsheet, or slides? What's your responsibility before you send it to anyone?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how to export artifacts and generated files, and why the final review before sharing always belongs to you.

modules.title: Connectors, MCP & Skills
modules.description: Extending what Claude can do — connectors, the Model Context Protocol, and installable skills.

questions.question: In plain terms, what is a connector (and the MCP standard behind it), and how does it change what Claude can do compared to a plain chat?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how connectors let Claude reach live tools and data through MCP, and what that adds beyond a plain chat.

questions.question: Before you enable a new connector — say, one that reads your email or files — what should you check or think through first?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review the questions to ask before granting a connector access: what it can read, what it can do, who built it, and whether your organization allows it.

questions.question: What is a skill in Claude, how do you install one, and when is a skill the right tool compared to just writing a good prompt?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review what skills package up, how to install one from a zip file, and which repeated workflows are worth turning into a skill.

modules.title: Everyday Workflows
modules.description: Reliable patterns for document review, research, analyzing files, and planning with Claude.

questions.question: Walk me through how you'd use Claude to review an important document before it goes out — what do you give it, what do you ask for, and what stays your job?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review the pattern of attaching the real document, asking risk-oriented questions, and keeping final judgment yourself.

questions.question: Describe a reliable workflow for researching a topic with Claude when accuracy matters. How do you handle sources and verification?
learningTopics: verifying claims before relying on them
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how to ground research in real sources, ask for citations, and verify anything you'll rely on or repeat.

questions.question: You have a spreadsheet of raw data and a question you want answered. How would you work through that with Claude, and how do you check the result before acting on it?
questions.focusAreaDetail: Review how to frame analysis questions, what context the data needs, and how to spot-check AI-produced numbers before acting on them.
